name: Chestnut Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Lundberg brown rice syrup (<1gm fat per tablespoon)
recipeInstructions:
1. Whip the egg whites until stiff peaks form.
2. Gradually add the brown rice syrup to the whipped egg whites, mixing gently until well combined. The mixture will become a bit flat as you incorporate the syrup.
3. In a separate bowl, mix together the flour and finely ground roasted chestnuts until well blended.
4. Gently fold the flour mixture into the egg white mixture until fully incorporated.
5. Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto parchment paper-lined cookie sheets or directly onto ungreased cookie sheets.
6. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for about 10 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
7. Allow cookies to cool on baking sheet for a few minutes before transferring them to wire racks to cool completely.
Comments: The cookies have a chewy texture and an old-fashioned flavor profile. They are not overly sweet unless additional brown rice syrup is added. Due to variations in the sweetness and flavor of the chestnuts used, each cookie may differ slightly in taste from the others.
